page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features dedicated CPU allocation. The CPU configuration provides 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring that each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical execution core without hyperthreading.

This instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include any local NVMe SSD storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage options. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance specifically for memory-bound workloads that do not require local high-speed scratch space or hardware acceleration.

With a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core, the ecs.r8i.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that require dedicated CPU performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, data buffering, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor at 2.7 GHz, the instance utilizes a KVM hypervisor and provides dedicated CPU allocation with 4 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications that require dedicated processing resources and a substantial memory footprint without the need for local storage or hardware accelerators.
